q: Two banks, A and B, offered loans at 3.5% and 7% per annum, respectively. Keshav borrowed an amount of Rs. 4,80,000 from each bank. Find the positive difference (in Rs.) between the amounts of simple interest paid to the two banks by Keshav after 2 years.
P is any point inside the rectangle ABCD. If PA = 86 cm, PB = 49 cm and PC = 79 cm, then the length of PD (in cm) is equal to:
A vehicle covers a distance of 1,200 km in 3 hours. What is the speed of the vehicle?

Amit and Shikha together invest a total amount of Rs. 46,800 in a business. At the end of the year, Amit's share of the profit is Rs. 8,750. If the total profit is Rs. 32,900, then what was Amit's investment?

A class of 69 students took a Physics test. 17 students had an average score of 94. The other students had an average score of 84. What is the average score (rounded off to one decimal place) of the whole class?

A cistern of dimensions 100 cm x 80 cm x 120 cm is fitted with an inlet pipe and an outlet pipe. The inlet pipe pours in 2000 cm^3 of water per minute and the outlet pipe can empty the full tank in 3 hours. If the tank is full and both pipes are open, how many hours will it take to empty the cistern?
If 30 men can complete a job in 11 days, then in how many days can 18 men complete 3/5 of that job?
If the price of a product is first increased by 18% and then decreased by 22%, find the percentage change in the price (correct to two places of decimals).
The marked price of a bed is Rs. 744, which is 36% above the cost price. If the profit percentage is 20%, find the discount percentage (rounded off to two decimal places).

The ratio of the price per unit of lemon chocolate to that of strawberry chocolate is 22 : 6, and a family consumes them in the ratio of 14 : 23 by quantity. Determine the ratio of the expenditure on strawberry chocolate to that on lemon chocolate.
The average age of 25 students of a class is 27 years. If the teacher's age is included, the average age of the class becomes 28 years. What is the age (in years) of the teacher?

A solid sphere of radius 7 cm is coated with a layer of paint that increases its radius to 7.5 cm, find the volume of the painted layer. (Use pi = 22/7 and round off your answer to the nearest integer)

A hollow metallic sphere with an external radius of 10 cm and an internal radius of 6 cm is melted and recast into a solid cone with a base radius of 8 cm. What is the height of the cone? (Use pi = 22/7)
A train of length 209 m takes 12 seconds to pass another train 101 m long coming from the opposite direction. If the speed of the first train is 71 km/hr, then find the speed of the second train.
A, B and C can complete a certain piece of work in 2, 5 and 20 minutes respectively. If they work together to complete that particular piece of work, how much time will they take?

The cost of a product is increased by Rs. 68,500 and the producer increases the price by 22.5%. Now if this increased price is further increased by 14%, then what will be the change in the price (in Rs.)?
A baker sells 100 cupcakes and earns a profit equal to the selling price of 20 cupcakes. What is his profit percentage?
Determine the value of: (sin15° / cos75°) + (cos78° / sin12°) - 1.
Five years ago, the ratio of the ages of two buildings was 7 : 2. Five years hence, the ratio will be 3 : 1. What is the present age of the newer building?
If a certain number is multiplied by 3, the result is 8 more than the product. What is the number?
If x^2 + 1/x^2 = 79, then what is the possible value of (x + 1/x)?
In a cinema, every 12 minutes 42 new viewers come to watch the film. If the total number of viewers in the cinema is to be 651, in how many minutes will it happen?

Seven boxes A, B, C, D, I, J and K are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. K is kept second from the bottom. Only three boxes are kept between A and I. D is kept at one of the positions below K. Only B is kept between I and J. A is kept at one of the positions above I. How many boxes are kept between C and D?
In a certain code, which set of letters will sit in place of the question mark (?) so that the series is fully meaningful and matches the pattern: IDR LGQ OJP RMO ?

A, B, C, D, W, X and Y are sitting around a circular table facing the centre of the table. Only four people sit between B and A when counted from the right of B. B sits third to the left of X. W sits third to the left of Y. C is an immediate neighbour of both A and X. How many people sit between D and C when counted from the right of D?
Seema is the 9th student from the front in her class row and the 19th student from the back. How many students are there in her row?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (6, 72, 12) (5, 60, 12)
Which number will sit in place of the question mark (?) so that the series follows a certain pattern: 5 8 14 23 35 50 ?
Which number will sit in place of the question mark (?) so that the series is fully meaningful and follows a certain pattern: 20 34 21 35 22 36 ?
In a certain code, 'LOVE' is coded as '4958' and 'VALE' is coded as '1589'. How is 'A' coded in that code?

This question is based on the five, three-digit numbers given below. (Left) 316 562 149 379 433 (Right) (Example: 697 – First digit = 6, second digit = 9 and third digit = 7) (NOTE: All operations to be done from left to right.) What will be the resultant if the second digit of the highest number is added to the third digit of the lowest number?

Raghu starts from Point Y and drives 18 km towards west. He then turns right and drives 26 km, takes a left turn, drives 30 km, turns left and drives 68 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 31 km. He takes a right turn, drives 42 km, turns left and drives 16 km to stop at Point Z. Now far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point Y again? (All turns are 90° turns only unless specified.)

Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. (Left) 7 * + 7 \ & 3 # 9 % 4 1 6 * @ 5 4 8 8 5 (Right) How many such numbers are there each of which is immediately preceded by another number and immediately followed by a symbol?

Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some books are novels. All novels are stories. Conclusions: (I): Some stories are books. (II): No books are stories. Which conclusion follows?

In a certain code language, 'A ? B' means 'A is the daughter of B', 'A + B' means 'A is the son of B', 'A # B' means 'A is the father of B' and 'A : B' means 'A is the sister of B'. How is L related to L if 'C ? D = E @ F \ L'?
If 'A' means '+', 'B' means '-', 'C' means 'x' and 'D' means '÷', then which symbol will sit in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation: 14 B 2 D 10 A 5 C 12 = ?

Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
In a certain code language, 'CORM' is coded as '9518' and 'MORE' is coded as '5831'. What is the code for 'E' in the given code language?

Arrange the given numbers in descending order and answer the question: (Given numbers) 7 $ 5 @ 3 ! 3 5 6 7 & % 5 3 # 2 7 + ? > < From the above numbers, in this newly arranged sequence, which symbol is the 3rd from the right end?
If '+' means '÷', '÷' means 'x', 'x' means '-' and '-' means '+', then which number will sit in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation: 12 - 15 + 143 x 98 x 7 = ?

P, Q, R, S, T and U are sitting in a row facing the north. S is sitting to the immediate left of Q. P is sitting to the immediate right of Q. U is sitting to the immediate right of P and immediate left of T. S and R are sitting at the ends of the line not necessarily in the same order. Who is sitting to the immediate right of T?
Which number will sit in place of the question mark (?) so that the series follows a certain pattern: 10, 23, 49, 88, 140, ?
